title: Algorithmic Trading
subtitle: Winning Strategies and Their Rationale
author: Ernest P. Chan
authorBio: Ernest P. Chan (PhD) is a quantitative finance researcher, author, and former hedge fund manager. He holds a PhD in physics from Cornell University and was a quantitative researcher at Morgan Stanley and IBM Research. He founded QTS Capital Management and has written several books on algorithmic and quantitative trading.
notableQuotes: A good trading strategy is like a good scientific hypothesis: it must be testable and falsifiable.
The biggest challenge in algorithmic trading is not strategy development but risk management.
Most trading strategies work for a while and then stop. The key is to have a diversified portfolio of strategies.
Backtesting is not research; it's engineering.
